facade_colour: white painted/clad ground floor storefront with dark glazed curtain wall above

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. With its visibly layered built form, the property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property has evolved over time while retaining original building features including its side gabled roofline. The property has contextual value because it supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. Summary: Reference Primary Address Entrance Address(es) Number 368. 14 St. Andrew Street N/A 196 | FO :D 30 & 32 St. Andrew Street (R-L)

facade_description: 14 St Andrew St presents a converted house-form, detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed (modern cladding and glass) in a white painted/clad ground floor storefront with dark glazed curtain wall above palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 3, 4 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. With its visibly layered built form, the property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property has evolved over time while retaining original building features including its side gabled roofline. The property has contextual value because it supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. Summary: Reference Primary Address Entrance Address(es) Number 368. 14 St. Andrew Street N/A 196 | FO :D 30 & 32 St. Andrew Street (R-L)
